subject: Ecology
title: Island Size and Extinction
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Island Size and Extinction.

hook.text: Small islands lose species faster because populations are tiny and have nowhere to retreat.

buildup.text: A hurricane, drought, or disease can wipe out an entire island population in one event.

discovery.text: Small populations also lose genetic diversity, making them less able to adapt.

twist.text: The smallest islands often hold the rarest species—unique but dangerously vulnerable.

climax.text: Many island extinctions happen silently—a species vanishes before science even names it.

punchline.text: Rarity and fragility share the same address.

quiz.question: Why do small islands have higher extinction rates?
options: Tiny populations are vulnerable to single events
